data.title: Coinbase proposes four suggestions calling on the US SEC to clarify digital asset regulation
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Hester Peirce, Commissioner of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), has solicited opinions in February on how the newly established cryptocurrency working group should deal with cryptocurrency issues. Faryar Shirzad, Chief Policy Officer of Coinbase, pointed out that Washington urgently needs to clarify the issue of cryptocurrency. The exchange has made four suggestions to the SEC, one of which aims to clarify that "digital assets that do not represent any corporate rights are not securities" but should be "digital commodities." Coinbase also asked the SEC to recognize that Congress should define "appropriate regulatory treatment of digital assets in the presence of ambiguity." However, the exchange stated in the document that stablecoins should be excluded from the jurisdiction of the SEC while Congress is formulating legislation. Coinbase's other two major suggestions include asking the SEC to clarify that "secondary market transactions in digital commodities are not securities transactions" and focusing on allowing stakeholders to tokenize traditional securities (such as tokenized debt). (Block)

data.title: The official contract address of Bedrock token will be announced after it goes live
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Bedrock announced the official contract address after its token $BR went online: 0xff7d6a96ae471bbcd7713af9cb1feeb16cf56b41
In addition, eligible users can now claim tokens. The claim window will open at 1:00 pm (UTC time) on March 20 and will last for 90 days. At the same time, $BR staking will be launched soon.
Bedrock officials remind the community to be wary of fake tokens. Be sure to verify the contract address through official channels before interacting.

data.title: FLock and Base reached a new cooperation, and the FLock model replaced Claude and connected to Base MCP
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The decentralized AI training platform FLock.io announced a new cooperation with the blockchain platform Base. The FLock Web3 Agent model will replace the centralized model Claude and connect to the Base MCP, bringing a new upgrade to on-chain interactions. This collaboration aims to address the current problems of complex blockchain operations and high privacy risks of centralized AI agents, and to provide users with smarter and more efficient on-chain services.
Through this cooperation, FLock's Web3 AI proxy model will support local operation on user devices without uploading data to a third party, ensuring privacy and security while achieving accurate function calls. With this solution, users can automate DeFi transactions, manage wallet assets, optimize gas fees, and quickly deploy smart contracts, significantly reducing the technical threshold of the on-chain economy and improving operational efficiency. Developers will also benefit from this, and the blockchain integration process will become simpler and more convenient.

data.title: Bitget launches Bedrock (BR)
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Bitget has launched Bedrock (BR) at 13:00 UTC time on March 20, trading pair: BR/USDT.

data.title: Aave founder: A proposal has been made to launch a new Aave staking module to enable AAVE repurchase and fee conversion
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Aave founder Stani.eth posted on the X platform that the community has initiated a proposal to launch a new Aave staking module to achieve AAVE repurchase and fee conversion.

data.title: ZKsync: During the protocol upgrade v26, there was a temporary problem with the Mailbox contract. It is recommended to migrate to BridgeHu
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News ZKsync issued a reminder on the X platform that during the protocol upgrade v26, there was a temporary problem with the backward compatibility of the Mailbox contract, which would affect L1 → L2 transactions. L1 → L2 transactions were originally handled by the Mailbox contract, but during the v26 upgrade, because the bridge and L2 were in different transition stages, backward compatibility did not work temporarily. 
It may take 1.5-2 weeks to solve this problem, so users are advised to use "requestL2TransactionDirect requestL2TransactionTwoBridges" on BridgeHub instead of Mailbox, and it is recommended to migrate to BridgeHub to avoid the Mailbox contract being deprecated in the future.

data.title: ZKsync Advises Users on Protocol Upgrade Compatibility Issues
data.description: According to Odaily, ZKsync has issued a notice on the X platform regarding a temporary compatibility issue during the protocol upgrade to version 26. The Mailbox contract, which initially handles L1 to L2 transactions, is experiencing backward compatibility problems due to the different transitional stages of bridging and L2 during the upgrade. This issue may take approximately 1.5 to 2 weeks to resolve. Users are advised to use 'requestL2TransactionDirect' and 'requestL2TransactionTwoBridges' on BridgeHub instead of Mailbox. Additionally, ZKsync recommends migrating to BridgeHub to avoid potential deprecation of the Mailbox contract in the future.

data.title: CoreWeave may go public with an IPO valuation of $30 billion
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the disclosed IPO documents, cloud computing provider CoreWeave may go public with an IPO valuation of $30 billion. It is reported that CoreWeave and Bitcoin mining company Core Scientific plan to invest $1.2 billion to expand the data center in Texas. The two parties have also reached a 12-year AI data center expansion agreement. (The Information)

data.title: BitMart will list Bedrock(BR)
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, BitMart will launch Bedrock (BR) at 22:00 (Eastern Time) on March 20, and the BR/USDT trading pair will be opened. BR is Bedrock's native governance token and forms the basis of its Proof of Stake Liquidity (PoSL) system, a framework that combines liquidity, governance and sustainable rewards.

data.title: Tether Emerges as Major U.S. Treasury Buyer
data.description: According to Odaily, Tether has become the seventh-largest holder of U.S. Treasury securities, with holdings amounting to $33.1 billion. This positions Tether alongside countries like the United Kingdom and Canada in terms of U.S. Treasury ownership.
